questionText: For a real number $x$, consider the statements $P:\ |x| < 3$ and $Q:\ -3 < x < 3$. Determine the logical relationship between $P$ and $Q$, proving both directions carefully.

steps.description: State the two statements and the goal.
steps.explanation: We want the correct connective between $P$ and $Q$, which means proving each implies the other or finding a counterexample. Writing both out keeps the two directions distinct.
The modulus measures how far $x$ sits from zero on the number line. Saying that distance is less than $3$ should mean $x$ lies strictly between $-3$ and $3$, which is what we now prove.

steps.description: Begin the forward direction by assuming P.
steps.explanation: To prove $P \Rightarrow Q$ we assume $|x|<3$ and split by the sign of $x$, because the modulus is defined piecewise. Handling each case removes the absolute-value symbol cleanly.

steps.description: Forward direction, case x is non-negative.
steps.explanation: When $x \ge 0$ the modulus is just $x$, so the assumption gives $x<3$. Combined with $x \ge 0$ this sits inside the interval $(-3,3)$.

steps.description: Forward direction, case x is negative.
steps.explanation: When $x<0$ the modulus equals $-x$, so $-x<3$ rearranges to $x>-3$. Together with $x<0$ this again lies inside $(-3,3)$.

steps.description: Combine the cases for the forward direction.
steps.explanation: Every real $x$ falls into one of the two cases, and each led to $-3<x<3$. Hence $|x|<3$ always forces $Q$.

steps.description: Begin the reverse direction by assuming Q.
steps.explanation: Now we prove $Q \Rightarrow P$ by starting from the double inequality. We again split on the sign of $x$ to evaluate $|x|$.

steps.description: Reverse direction, case x is non-negative.
steps.explanation: For $x \ge 0$ the right half of $Q$ already gives $x<3$, and $|x|=x$, so the modulus is below $3$ directly.

steps.description: Reverse direction, case x is negative.
steps.explanation: For $x<0$ the left half of $Q$ gives $x>-3$; multiplying by $-1$ flips it to $-x<3$. Since $|x|=-x$ here, the modulus is again below $3$.

steps.description: Combine the cases for the reverse direction.
steps.explanation: Both sign cases produced $|x|<3$, so the interval condition always forces $P$. The reverse implication is established.

steps.description: Combine both directions into the final relationship.
steps.explanation: Since each statement implies the other, they are logically equivalent. This is exactly why $|x|<3$ is standard shorthand for $-3<x<3$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $P \Leftrightarrow Q$: $|x| < 3$ if and only if $-3 < x < 3$. Both directions hold, so the statements are logically equivalent.
